sections.instruction: Complete the notes below and the table below, and choose the correct letter A, B or C as instructed.

questions.content: Traveller’s cheques can be replace within {{BLANK}}
The bank will buy back unused traveller’s cheques
options: A free of charge
B for a small fee
C if they are returned in good condition

questions.content: Sandra does not want Euros because she
options: A already has some
B will be given some
C will be buying some at the airport
A collect the cheques from the branch
B be at work
C sign for the cheques at home

sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B or C, complete the notes, choose TWO letters A-E, and write the correct letter A, B or C next to 18-20.

options: A are offered free to all students
B are recongnised internationally
C give you a year’s worth of discounts

questions.content: Student Railcards • For young people between 16 and 25 • Mature students 25+ studying in {{BLANK}} • Get {{BLANK}} train fares in the UK • 1 year £ {{BLANK}} • 3 years {{BLANK}}
Local bus operators offer bus passes
options: A especially for students
B for various lengths of time
C that are cheaper the longer the period
D for use only for commuting
E that can be used by your friends

questions.content: Which places are useful for the following? 18. the university campus {{BLANK}} 19. charity shops {{BLANK}} 20. car boot sales {{BLANK}}
options: A Household items
B Vintage clothes
C Second-hand textbooks

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below and choose the correct letter A, B or C.

options: A significantly reduce the amount of paper it uses
B be careful about the amount of documents it produces
C discover why students keep losing their work

questions.content: According to the text, students at the college
options: A are not allowed to print documents
B have restrictions on how many pages they can print
C have to pay to do any printing

B wants the students to give them feedback about the quizzes
C thinks the online quizzes are more effective than the paper ones

sections.instruction: Complete the sentences below, answer the questions by writing E or P, and choose the correct letter A, B or C.

questions.content: 31. {{BLANK}} times are less common within the family 32. We may eat more than we need due to our {{BLANK}} 33. Eating habits can be affected by both {{BLANK}} emotions. 34. People who are {{BLANK}} are more likely to eat more due to negative emotions.

Which type of hunger relates to the points below? 35. Experiences a sudden urge to eat {{BLANK}} 36. Hungers for any type of food {{BLANK}} 37. Need to satisfy the hunger as soon as possible {{BLANK}} 38. Will stop eating once full {{BLANK}} 39. Will feel no sense of guilt after eating {{BLANK}}
options: E emotional hunger
P physical hunger

questions.content: People suffering from obesity can be helped by
options: A taking the pressure off them about feeling guilty
B understanding why they have negative feelings
C pinpointing stressful moments that can cause emotional hunger
